name: Chinese-Style Chicken (4 Servings)
recipeIngredient: 3/8 lb to 2 lb chicken pieces; meaty breast halves, thighs or drumsticks
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove skin from chicken.
2. Rinse the chicken and pat dry.
3. Spray a cold large skillet with nonstick cooking spray.
4. Preheat the skillet over medium heat.
5. Brown chicken pieces on all sides in the hot skillet.
6. Add 3/4 cup water, sherry, soy sauce, and garlic powder to the skillet.
7. Bring to boiling; reduce heat.
8. Simmer, covered, for 35 to 40 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.
9. Transfer chicken to a serving platter and keep warm.
10. For sauce, stir together the remaining 2 tablespoons water and cornstarch in a small bowl.
11. Cook and stir celery for 3 to 4 minutes or until crisp and tender.
12. Stir in the cornstarch mixture; cook and stir until thickened and bubbly.
13. Cook and stir for an additional 2 minutes.
14. Serve chicken and sauce with hot cooked rice. Makes 4 servings.
Food Exchange per serving: 3 LEAN MEAT EXCHANGES + 1 STARCH/BREAD EXCHANGE
Source: Better Homes and Gardens Diabetic Cookbook
Brought to you and yours via Nancy O'Brion and her Meal-Master.
